sourceMovie.title: Felon
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Felon (2008) stuck because it commits to its crime premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Ric Roman Waugh's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Faster
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Felon clicked for you, Faster (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Felon?
faq.answer: Try Faster, Lock Up, Undisputed II: Last Man Standing—each shares DNA with Felon while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Felon?
faq.answer: Felon (2008) is best described as Crime, Drama, Thriller, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Felon worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
